hypothesis: Fine-tuning chemical language models with an equivariance-inducing regularization that penalizes changes in token-embedding-derived molecular representations under rotations of their corresponding 3D conformers will increase their CKNNA and IDCor alignment with equivariant 3D MLIPs and produce significant, measurable improvements in zero-shot and few-shot prediction accuracy on 3D-dependent properties (e.g., DFT total energy, NMR chemical shifts), independent of model size and training-data overlap.
